content:
The wood of the bench is cool tonight, but the keys have already taken on the warmth of my blood. It’s 11:45 PM on a Saturday. The crowd at Sheba’s is a mix of the ghosts I’ve always known and the new faces who look at their screens to find out where they are, even while they’re sitting right here.

I don’t mind the phones as much as I used to. They’re just another kind of glass, and glass always
